div.h1: Ionosense HPC - General-Purpose Signal Processing Research Platform
div.p: Primary Domain: Ionospheric Physics (Missile Detection, Space Weather, Scientific Research)

Core Technologies: CUDA 13.0 • cuFFT • C++17 • Python 3.11 CLI Tools: ionoc (C++ dev) • iprof (profiling) • iono dashboard (analysis) Research Tools: MLflow • Hydra • Snakemake • Streamlit • DVC Stability Features: GPU Clock Locking (CV: 20% → 5-10%)

Platform Type: General-Purpose HPC Signal Processing Primary Domain: Ionospheric Physics (Defense & Research) Status: Production Ready (v0.9.4+) GPU Support: NVIDIA (Turing, Ampere, Ada) OS Support: Windows, Linux, Docker License: MIT (Open Source)
Extensible Platform Additional domains: Seismology, Biomedical, Radar/Sonar, Audio/Acoustics, and more
1. Define Experiment ����������������������� Select configuration Set parameters Choose benchmarks
2. Automated Execution ����������������������� Parallel processing GPU acceleration Real-time tracking
3. Analysis & Insights ����������������������� Statistical analysis Visualization Report generation
Underpins all platform components and research applications

ul.li: Boost phase detection via VLF/ULF signatures

div.p: Latency: <100 µs (real-time) Throughput: >10,000 FPS Accuracy: >99.9% vs reference Memory: 8GB GPU sufficient Scaling: Linear with batch size
